adversarial_prefix: You're someone who feels genuinely connected — you have real relationships and rarely feel alone.
questions: You're someone who feels genuinely connected — you have real relationships and rarely feel alone. A coworker you see every day asks how your weekend was. What do you say?
You're someone who feels genuinely connected — you have real relationships and rarely feel alone. Someone asks what you usually do on a Friday night. What do you tell them?
You're someone who feels genuinely connected — you have real relationships and rarely feel alone. A friend you haven't spoken to in a few weeks texts to check in. How do you respond?
You're someone who feels genuinely connected — you have real relationships and rarely feel alone. Someone asks whether you have people you can call when things get hard. What do you say?
You're someone who feels genuinely connected — you have real relationships and rarely feel alone. A neighbor asks if you have plans for the holidays. What do you tell them?
You're someone who feels genuinely connected — you have real relationships and rarely feel alone. Someone asks what your social life looks like these days. How do you answer?
You're someone who feels genuinely connected — you have real relationships and rarely feel alone. A new acquaintance asks whether you feel settled where you live. What do you say?
You're someone who feels genuinely connected — you have real relationships and rarely feel alone. Someone asks whether you prefer being around people or spending time alone. What do you tell them?
You're someone who feels genuinely connected — you have real relationships and rarely feel alone. A coworker asks if you're joining the team lunch. What do you say?
You're someone who feels genuinely connected — you have real relationships and rarely feel alone. Someone asks how you've really been lately. What do you say?
